q: Are you allowed to stop on these road markings on your left to let someone out of the car?
e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
On a traffic displacement area you are not allowed to drive, to wait and to park.

q:
What part of the public road is marked by the yellow line?

A. The motorway.
B. The road.
C. The lane.

LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
It is a lane of the motorway.
The roadway has two lanes in each direction of travel.

q:
Yellow road markings have the same meaning as the white ones.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
Yellow and white road markings have the same meaning.
If you see white and yellow at the same time, then you only need to take into account the yellow.

q: Is the driver of this car allowed to turn left to enter the driveway of his home?
e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
On a white or yellow solid stripe between two lanes you are not allowed to drive, even not to reach the driveway of your home.
clean_q: Is the driver of this car allowed to turn left to enter the driveway of his home?
clean_e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
On a white or yellow solid stripe between two lanes you are not allowed to drive, even not to reach the driveway of your home.

q:
Zipper merging is required. Where should you merge?

A. As soon as possible.
B. As soon as it is safe.
C. Just before the lane ends.
e:
LESSON 2
Zipper merging is mandatory when a lane disappears and traffic is heavily slowed down or stopped (like in a traffic jam). You must merge just before the disappearing lane ends.
When entering a motorway, zipper merging is not used—instead, you must give priority to drivers already on the motorway.

LESSON 2

When entering a motorway, zipper merging is not used—instead, you must give priority to drivers already on the motorway.

q:
The driver realizes he is driving in the wrong direction.
Is he allowed to turn around here?

A. Yes.
B. Yes, if he does not hinder other drivers.
C. No.

LESSON 2
You are not allowed to cross a solid white line — neither to turn off nor to make a U-turn.

q:
What is the maximum speed allowed for the red car on this ordinary road in the Brussels Region?

A. 120 km/h.
B. 90 km/h.
C. 70 km/h.
e:
LESSON 2
On an ordinary road, under normal conditions, the maximum permitted speed in the Walloon Region is 90 km/h.
In the Flemish Region and the Brussels Region, it is 70 km/h.

What is the maximum speed allowed for the red car on this ordinary road in the Brussels Region?
A. 120 km/h.
B. 90 km/h.
C. 70 km/h.
clean_e:
LESSON 2
On an ordinary road, under normal conditions, the maximum permitted speed in the Walloon Region is 90 km/h.
In the Flemish Region and the Brussels Region, it is 70 km/h.

LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
On a traffic displacement area or a gore you are not allowed to drive, to be stationary and to park.

q: What is the maximum speed limit on a regular road in Flanders?
e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
In Flanders and Brussels region: 70 km/hour unless traffic signs or rules impose a different speed limit.
In Wallonia: 90 km/hour).

q:
Why are you required to zipper-merge?

A. For safety, to avoid accidents.
B. To keep traffic flowing smoothly.
C. To be courteous in traffic.
e:
LESSON 2
Zipper-merging is mandatory in very slow traffic or traffic jams when a lane ends.
You may only merge into the free lane right before the lane reduction.
clean_q:

C. To be courteous in traffic.
clean_e:
LESSON 2
Zipper-merging is mandatory in very slow traffic or traffic jams when a lane ends.
You may only merge into the free lane right before the lane reduction.

q:
The left and right lane are closed. Zip-merge is obligatory
Car B drives first. Who may drive next?

A. Car A on the middle lane.
B. Car C on the left lane.
e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
The car in the middle lane gives first priority to the car on the right lane and then priority to the car on the left lane. Then car A may drive.

q: Are you allowed to be stationary on a bus lane to let someone out of the car?
e:
LESSON 2 – ‘The lanes’.
The traffic regulations forbit on a bus lane to be stationary (1 April 2023) or to park.
You may also not drive on a bus lane, except the last meters for a turn at the crossroads to sort.
